description: Outside a cafe with a green storefront, Monica stands on roller skates, holding onto the window frame. She wears a red shirt, grey pants, black wrist guards, and kneepads, while Phoebe approaches wearing a yellow top and skirt.
Monica turns to Phoebe, gesturing with one hand as she asks, "So whose idea was it to put everybody in the diner on skate?"
Phoebe stops walking and replies, "Some idiot customer put a suggestion in the suggestion box."
Phoebe places her hands near her face and says, "Oh, my God. They took my idea." Monica looks at her and asks, "That was you?" Phoebe replies, "Yeah."
Monica keeps her gaze on Phoebe.
Monica lowers her hands and turns away, saying, "Okay."
A woman with long hair, wearing denim overalls and a yellow t-shirt, stands near the cafe door. Monica skates toward her holding a cup, saying, "There you go, Rachel. I made you a"
Monica collides with Rachel, and both fall backward onto the sidewalk with a thud. Monica lands on top of Rachel, and a passing man in a blue shirt drops his cup, which splatters on the ground.
Phoebe and the man step toward the fallen women. The man bends forward slightly and asks, "god. Are you guys okay?"
Phoebe bends over Monica and Rachel. Joey steps out of the cafe holding a cup, looks down at them, and speaks briefly. Monica and Rachel remain on the ground.
Joey looks down at them with a slight smile. The screen goes black.
The screen remains completely black.
The setting changes to an outdoor view of a city skyline at dusk.
Inside an apartment, Joey sits in a black recliner, aiming a remote at a television. Chandler enters from a hallway, wearing a green shirt and a tan vest.
Chandler walks toward the kitchen area, stopping to look at Joey. Both men speak briefly without subtitles.
Chandler looks at Joey and says, "Oh, so that's why the priest threw holy water on me."
Joey remains seated in the recliner, his gaze directed downward.
Chandler gestures with his hand as he says to Joey, "Okay, listen, you have to cheer up, okay? You should come out with Ross and me. I mean, anything is"
Chandler continues gesturing, adding, "better than sitting around here crying all day about Kate." Joey turns his head toward Chandler.
Joey raises a hand, pointing a finger toward the television as he says, "Hey, I was crying because nobody believed"
Joey lowers his hand and looks back at the screen, finishing, "Quincy's theory, okay?"
Ross walks into the apartment wearing an orange button-down shirt.
Ross walks further inside, smiling, and says, "Hey, I'm gonna be on TV." Chandler turns to him and responds, "No way."
Ross gestures as he continues, "Yeah. They're putting together this panel to talk about these fossils they just found"
Ross finishes his sentence, "in Peru. And the Discovery Channel is going to film it." Chandler and Joey laugh. Chandler looks at Ross and says, "Oh, my God."
Chandler maintains his smile as he asks, "Who's going to watch that?"
Ross's smile fades as he looks at Chandler.
Ross lowers his head slightly, then looks back at Chandler and asks, "Thanks. You ready to go?"
Joey looks at Chandler from the recliner and says, "Yeah. Saw a girl with that vest."
Chandler walks past the back of Joey's chair toward the door.
Chandler turns and replies, "Thanks."
Chandler and Ross walk out the door. Joey places the remote on the counter, then picks up a cordless phone and a phone book.

The television shows a female news anchor speaking, then switches to footage of baby chicks walking on straw. The anchor's voice says, "While most of us think of chocolate bunnies and baskets as traditional Easter gifts". Joey opens the phone book on his lap.
Joey looks down at the phone book. The television audio continues, "some people insist on giving live chicks as" while showing the chicks.
The television audio finishes the sentence, "presents." over footage of people's hands reaching toward the chicks.
Joey looks toward the television with a slight smile, holding the phone to his ear.

Joey's slight smile fades as he listens to the television, the anchor now visible on the screen saying, "Unfortunately, the sad fact remains that most of these little guys won't live to see the 4 July".
Joey looks down at the phone book, turning a page. The television audio continues over footage of the chicks, "because as a result of improper care,"
The television shows a close-up of the chicks as the anchor's voice finishes, "they will be dead."
Joey's brow furrows as he holds the phone to his ear.
Joey speaks into the phone, "Yeah. Hi. You guys got any of those baby chicks?"
Joey gestures with his free hand and adds, "Because I was watching this commercial on TV and, man, those things are cute."
The setting changes to an exterior daytime view of a diner with a sign reading "Moondance Diner."
Inside the diner, Monica, wearing a blonde wig, a pink cardigan, and a grey skirt, stands behind the counter as Pete walks toward her.
Pete approaches the counter, smiling, and says, "Hi." Monica smiles and replies, "Hi, Pete."
Monica steps out from behind the counter, gesturing for Pete to look at her, and says, "You're back. Hey, check this out."
Monica moves forward on roller skates toward Pete.
Pete steps forward and says, "Wow."
Monica skates into Pete, and he catches her by the arms, saying, "Skates."
Pete wraps his arms around Monica, and she hugs him in return.
Pete smiles, keeping his arms around Monica, and says, "Wow, you're a lot sturdier than Chandler."
Monica pulls back from the hug and says, "You crumpled like a piece of paper. How's your trip?" Pete reaches into his pocket and pulls out a small paper gift bag.
Monica points at the bag and asks, "What'd you bring?"
Pete hands the gift bag to Monica.
Monica looks inside the bag, pulls out two small bottles, and says, "Aww, hotel toiletries from Japan."
Monica holds the two small bottles in front of her.
Monica smiles at Pete and says, "These are going to go in my permanent collection."
Pete touches the label on one of the bottles Monica is holding.
Pete points at the label and says, "You know what that says in English? Made in Texas."
Monica laughs and lowers the bottles.
Monica looks at Pete and asks, "You want some coffee?" Pete nods and replies, "Yeah, sure. That'd be great."
Monica turns and skates behind the counter. Pete stands in place, watching her.
Monica leans her arms on the counter, smiling, and asks, "Regular, decaf." Pete responds, "Whichever is closest."
Monica turns around to prepare the coffee.
Monica places a cup of coffee on the counter and rests her hands over Pete's. Pete says, "So ask me what I did today." Monica asks, "So, what did you do today, Pete?"
Pete looks at Monica and says, "I bought a restaurant. And I would like you to be the head chef." Monica's eyes widen, and she asks, "What?"
Monica parts her lips and says, "Oh,".

The clip begins with an exterior view of a corner apartment building at night. The setting changes to the inside of an apartment. Monica stands near the open refrigerator holding a bottle, and Rachel stands nearby holding a notebook.

Monica gestures with the bottle and says, "Can you believe he just offered me a restaurant?" Rachel drops her notebook onto a chair and responds, "What a jerk. Want me to kick his ass?" Audience laughter is audible.
Monica shakes her head.
Walking toward the kitchen area, Monica gestures with both hands and says, "This has been, like, my dream since I got my first Easy Bake Oven and opened Easy Monica's Bakery."
Monica reaches the counter, sets the bottle down, and adjusts her hair. Rachel walks past her toward the stove.
Rachel picks up a yellow kettle. Monica adjusts her earring and stands at the counter.
Monica gestures as she says, "I mean, I would kill for this job. I mean, I can totally do this job. And God knows I paid my dues." Rachel pours from the kettle into a mug.
Rachel finishes pouring and stirs the mug with a spoon, while Monica continues gesturing toward her.

Monica says, "Pete's just doing this because he has a crush on me." Taking a sip from her mug, Rachel asks, "You're still not attracted to him at all?" Monica answers, "No," followed by audience laughter.
Monica shakes her head.
Gesturing with one hand, Monica says, "I mean, how can I accept a restaurant from him?"

Monica says, "I can't. I couldn't even accept a necklace from Stu Vincent in the 7th." Rachel replies, "Damn, Mon, that was totally different. He was your health teacher." Audience laughter is audible.
Rachel winces, suddenly grips her side, and lets out an audible gasp.
Monica looks at Rachel and asks, "What? Honey?"

Gripping her side, Rachel says, "Oh, my side still hurts from when you crashed into me yesterday." Monica pulls her into a hug and says, "Oh, God. I'm so sorry." Rachel winces and says, "Oh, God," over Monica's shoulder.
Monica and Rachel separate from the hug.
Audience cheering and laughter are audible as Ross enters the apartment wearing a white suit with a red bow tie. He says, "Hey, you guys. Guess what?"
Ross walks to the counter and stops. Monica and Rachel stand side by side, staring at him.
Looking at Ross's suit, Monica says, "Got a job on a riverboat."
Ross stares at Monica as audience laughter plays.

Ross looks at Rachel, points at himself, and says, "You know what? I didn't wear this suit for a year because you hated it. Well, guess what? You're not my girlfriend anymore." Rachel begins to reply, "Oh, I"
Rachel gestures toward Ross and says, "see. So this suit is making a point now that you're on your own, you're free to look as stupid as you'd like."
Ross continues to stare at Rachel.
Ross turns toward Monica and asks, "You like it, right?" Monica nods and replies, "Oh, absolutely. I like it even more on you than I did on Colonel Sanders."
Ross turns around and steps toward the door.
Monica says, "Ross, I'm kidding." Ross replies, "Yeah, I'm here." Monica asks, "What were you going to tell us?" Rachel adds, "Yeah."
Rachel asks, "Oh, was it how you invented the cotton gin" Ross turns at the door, says, "goodbye," and walks out, pulling the door closed. Audience laughter overlaps his exit.
Monica holds her hands to her face, laughing. Rachel laughs beside her.
Rachel stops laughing and asks, "Was that a little harsh?" Monica replies, "Oh, come on. Are you kidding? Don't you remember when you were kids and you used to wear that gray"
Monica gestures as she finishes, "sweatsuit and they used to call you the Hindenburg?"
Rachel stares at Monica.
Rachel says, "No."
Monica picks up a mug and takes a sip, her eyes shifting downward.
Lowering the mug, Monica says, "My mistake." Audience laughter is audible.
Monica turns her back toward Rachel.

The setting changes to Chandler and Joey's apartment. Chandler sits on a stool at the kitchen counter, and Phoebe stands near the refrigerator. Chandler asks, "So after you put the suggestion in the box, how long did it take for the roller skating thing to happen?"
Holding a tin container, Phoebe replies, "About three months." Chandler nods and says, "Okay, so I guess that's about two weeks before the topless thing kicks in." Audience laughter follows.
Phoebe touches her chin. The apartment door opens, and Joey walks in carrying a shoebox. Audience cheering is audible.
Joey smiles and says, "Hey,"
Joey walks toward the counter, holding the box.
Joey approaches the counter and says, "something."
Joey places the shoebox on the counter in front of Chandler and Phoebe.
Tapping the top of the box, Joey says, "Open it. Open it."
Chandler leans forward and reaches for the shoebox.
Chandler says, "Okay."
Chandler lifts the lid off the box, revealing a small yellow chick inside.
Chandler stares into the box. Joey and Phoebe smile.
Looking up at Joey, Chandler says, "It's a chicken."
Joey grins, and Phoebe looks down into the box.
Leaning over the box, Phoebe says, "Cute."
Chandler looks back and forth between Joey and Phoebe, asking, "Whoa, whoa, whoa. You guys. Do you know anything about chicks?"
Joey shakes his head and says, "Fowl no."
Chandler looks at Joey steadily.
Chandler asks, "Women?" Joey shakes his head and replies, "No."
Phoebe looks at Joey as Chandler shakes his head slightly.
Phoebe gestures with her hands and says, "They are a huge responsibility, especially at this age. They require constant care. They need just the right food and"
Phoebe finishes, "lots and lots of love."
Joey reaches into the box.
Lifting the chick out of the box, Joey says, "Well, no problem."
Joey cradles the chick against his chest with both hands and lightly kisses it. Phoebe smiles at him. Audience "aww" sounds and laughter are audible.
Joey continues to hold the chick close to his chest. Chandler watches him.
Chandler stares at Joey and says, "Easy, Lenny."
Joey clutches the chick to his chest, keeping his eyes on Chandler. The setting changes to a daytime exterior view of a city street crowded with moving traffic.
In a diner, Pete sits at the counter while Monica stands facing him. Phoebe is seated at the counter in the background. Pete says, "So, I mean, have you thought about it?"
Monica looks down slightly.
Monica says, "Okay, here's the thing." Pete interrupts, shaking his head, "Oh, no, not the thing. I hate the thing."
In the background, Phoebe leans forward and asks, "What's the thing?"
Monica turns her head briefly.
Monica looks at Pete and says, "I can't do it. I'm sorry."
"I wish I could," Monica adds.
Monica says, "But see, you have these feelings for" before Pete interrupts with his hand raised, "wait, that's what you're worried about? If that's the problem, we've got no."
Monica looks at him.
"Huh?" Monica asks. Pete says, "No. Like, I was going to tell you this over dinner, but I met somebody else on my trip."
"Oh?" Monica says. Pete continues, "Her name's Anne. She's a journalist. We met on the plane. She asked me if she could finish off my peanuts. I thought she"
Pete smiles and finishes, "said something else. We had a big" Audience laughter begins.
Both Pete and Monica smile. Audience laughter continues.
Pete says, "I mean, I got tired of waiting."
Monica smiles and says, "Well, that's great. I mean, I'm sorry, but I'm so happy for you."
Monica says, "And now I can work for you." Pete replies, "I guess you can." Monica gasps, "Oh my God. Oh, this is incredible," and steps forward.
Monica embraces Pete.
Releasing the embrace, Monica says, "All right, you know what? I'm just going to roll right in that office and quit." Pete says, "All right."
Monica points toward the door and asks, "Could you give me a little push?" Pete replies, "Yeah,"
Pete pushes Monica's waist, and she rolls backward on roller skates. Pete says, "sure. Good luck." Monica says, "I'm quitting." Audience laughter plays.
Rolling off-screen through the doorway, Monica yells, "Woohoo." A loud crash is heard.
Pete and the other diner patrons turn to look toward the door as audience laughter peaks.
Monica calls out from off-screen, "I'm okay. I'm alright."
Pete walks around the counter to sit. Phoebe turns her head to watch him.
Phoebe says, "Wow, that's exciting."
Pete turns to face Phoebe.
Phoebe continues, "You went to Japan, made up a woman."
Pete says, "What?" Phoebe stirs her drink and says, "I'm just saying, this woman, I mean, she's fictitious, no?"
Pete looks at her.
Pete asks, "And why would you say that?" Phoebe answers, "Because you're still into Monica. So you told her there was somebody else so she"
Phoebe continues, gesturing with her spoon, "would agree to work with you because you figure, oh, if we spend a lot of time together, maybe something might happen."
Pete stares at Phoebe.
Pete points at her and says, "You're good. You're good."
Phoebe smiles.
Phoebe says, "Yeah. No, I'm fairly intuitive and psychic."
Pete looks down slightly and says, "It's a substantial gift."
Pete lifts a spoon to his mouth.
Pete says, "Listen, can you promise me that you won't tell her though?" Phoebe replies, "Absolutely."
Phoebe adds, "Oh, I promise."
Pete nods.
Phoebe asks, "Tell her what?"
Audience laughter plays.
Pete stares at her and says, "Thanks a lot." Phoebe replies, "No, I'm serious. I mean, I'm intuitive, but my memory sucks."
The setting changes to an apartment, showing a chick inside a small wooden pen on a counter.
Chandler, seated near the counter, leans forward and sings, "Okay, but this is the last time. With a chick chick here and a chick chick there here a chick there a chick"
Chandler finishes singing, "everywhere chick chick chicken" as Joey opens the apartment door and walks in.
Joey closes the door, lowers his bag from his shoulder, and approaches the counter.
Joey bends to look at the pen and says, "Hey. How's she doing?"
Chandler looks at Joey.
Chandler replies, "She well, yeah. Don't you think it's a she?" Joey says, "I don't know," and picks up the chick.
Joey holds the chick in one hand, examining its underside with his thumb. Chandler watches him.
Joey continues to inspect the chick while squinting his eyes.
Joey says, "I can't tell. Whatever it was went back in too quickly." Audience laughter is heard.
Joey places the chick back into the pen.
Joey turns away and says, "Anyway, I got to go change. I'm meeting some of the cast for drinks." Chandler says, "Excuse me."
Joey turns back and asks, "What?"
Chandler leans forward.
Chandler says, "I stayed home from work today while you were at rehearsal. So somebody could be here with our chick." Joey interjects, "Hey."
Joey gestures to himself and says, "Who was up from 02:00 this morning until 05:00 this morning trying to get her back to sleep."
Chandler gestures with his hand.
Chandler asks, "You don't think I get up when you get up?"
Joey nods.
Joey says, "Here it comes."
Chandler stands up, gesturing broadly, and says, "Yes, here it comes. I'm stuck here all day and then you come in and spend 2 seconds with"
Chandler finishes, "us"
Joey stares at Chandler.
Chandler adds, his voice rising, "and then expect to go off gallivanting with your friends?"
Joey crosses his arms.
Joey shakes his head and says, "Well, I don't think so, Mr."
Joey uncrosses his arms and steps forward.
Joey raises his hands and says, "Hey, I need to relax, okay? I was working all day."
Chandler looks at him.
Chandler asks loudly, "And you don't think taking care of our chick is work?"
Joey sighs, turns his back, and takes a few steps toward the hallway.
Joey turns back and says, "That's not what I said, okay? I just meant—" Chandler interrupts, speaking louder, "I know what you meant."
Joey looks at Chandler with his mouth slightly open, while Chandler stares back with a tight jaw. Audience laughter fades out.
In an apartment kitchen and living area, Joey stands with his hands on his hips, sighing and looking away. Chandler sits on a stool near the counter.
Chandler says, "You noticed that ever since we got this chick, we've been fighting a lot more than we used to." Joey looks at him, turns, and walks away.
Joey stops and crosses his arms, looking down.
Joey says, "I don't know. Maybe we weren't ready to have a chick."
Chandler looks down, then back at Joey.
Chandler says, "I sure want one someday, though." Joey replies, "I know."
Joey nods slightly, his arms still crossed.
"I'll take her back tomorrow," Joey says.
Chandler looks at Joey.
"You think we'll get our $3 back?" Chandler asks.
The door opens and Ross enters, carrying a black suit bag over his shoulder.
"Hey," Ross says.
"Oh, hey," Joey and Chandler reply simultaneously.
Ross walks further into the room. Chandler stands up.

Ross points at Chandler's face and says, "Can't believe you still have that." Chandler replies, "We're taking her back tomorrow." Ross says, "No, that ridiculous thing on your face." Joey points at Ross, saying, "Thank you." Chandler says, "Right."
Ross sets down the bag, unzipping it. "Listen, I had that TV thing in like, 2 hours and I need your help. Okay. What do you think?"
Ross pulls two suits on hangers out of the bag.
Ross holds up a blue suit and a brown suit. "This blue suit or this brown one?" Chandler says, "Well, the brown one brings out your eyes, but your butt looks great in the blue one."
"It" Chandler begins.
Ross turns to look at Chandler with his eyebrows furrowed.
"really" Ross says.
The setting changes to another apartment. Rachel, wearing a bathrobe, walks into the kitchen area holding her right side and grimacing.
"ow," Rachel says softly, wincing as she reaches the counter.
Rachel opens a pill bottle on the counter, taps a pill into her hand, and puts it in her mouth.
Rachel picks up a water bottle, takes a sip, and turns around, stepping stiffly while holding her side. The front door opens and Ross enters carrying his suit bag.
"Wow," Ross says.
"That aspirin dance really works," Ross says.
Rachel walks stiffly to a chair, leaning on it with one hand while holding her side.
Ross steps closer. "Oh, my God. Is that still" Rachel interrupts, "I'm fine. I'm fine." Ross says, "No, you're not." Rachel replies, "I am." Ross says, "Rach."
"Look, I'm fine. Watch. Look at" Rachel lifts one leg.
Rachel loses her balance and groans, grabbing her side. Ross steps forward and grabs her arm to steady her.
Ross holds her arm. "okay, okay. Look, you've got to go to a doctor, okay?" Rachel pulls away, "No, I have got to get ready and go to a dinner at my boss's"
"house. It's a very big deal. There's a lot of people there that I have to meet," Rachel continues. Ross gestures with his hands, saying, "Yeah, and I'm sure you're going to make a great"
"impression. Hi, I'm Rachel Green," Ross finishes, lifting one leg stiffly and leaning back with a pained expression.
"It's nice to meet you," Ross says, sticking out his hand to shake while maintaining the stiff posture.
Ross lowers his leg. Rachel turns and slowly walks away.
"Come on. You probably have a broken rib," Ross says following her. "Well, I will go to the hospital tomorrow," Rachel replies.
"It
Rachel says, "But you know, I could use a hand getting ready." Ross says, "Rachel," and Rachel says, "look, either help me or go," extending her arms.
Ross throws his hands down and turns around.
"Fine, I'll go," Ross says.
Rachel stands still, looking at him.
"Okay. But before you go, could you help me first?" Rachel asks.
Ross stops at the door, turns back, checks his watch, and sighs.
"Sure, I'll help," Ross says, walking towards her.
Chandler bursts through the front door.
Chandler, gesturing frantically, says, "Good. Good. Do you guys know how to get a chick out of a VC"
The screen cuts to black.
The screen remains black.
